room_types.name: Executive King
room_types.description: 28 m² room on floors 7-9 with Tagus river views and late check-out until 14:00 included.

room_types.name: Business Suite
room_types.description: 45 m² river-view suite with a separate sitting room and six-seat meeting table — a working office that sleeps three.
features: separate sitting room with six-seat meeting table
sofa bed for a third guest

facts.question: What time can I check in?
facts.answer: Check-in opens at 15:00. The front desk is staffed 24 hours, so there is no cutoff — arrive whenever your day allows.

facts.question: When do I have to check out?
facts.answer: Check-out is by 12:00. Use the lobby express kiosk or simply drop your key card; the invoice is emailed automatically. Executive King guests have late check-out until 14:00 included.

facts.question: I land late — can I check in at 2 or 3 in the morning?
facts.answer: Yes. Reception is staffed around the clock, so you check in normally at the desk at any hour. Your room is held all night against the card on the booking.

facts.question: Can I check in before 15:00?
facts.answer: Early check-in from 11:00 costs EUR 30, subject to availability; to guarantee it, book the previous night. Luggage storage is free if your room isn't ready.

facts.question: Do you have parking?
facts.answer: Yes, an underground garage on site: EUR 18 per night, no reservation needed, entrance on Rua do Bojador, height limit 1.90 m. Two 22 kW EV chargers at EUR 0.35 per kWh.

facts.question: What time is breakfast and how much does it cost?
facts.answer: Buffet breakfast in the Tejo restaurant runs 06:30-10:30 on weekdays and 07:00-11:00 on weekends; EUR 16 per person, or EUR 12 when added to the booking. Grab-and-go bags are ready from 05:00 if requested the night before.

facts.question: Can I bring my dog?
facts.answer: No pets, I'm afraid; certified assistance dogs are the exception and stay free.

facts.question: Is there Wi-Fi?
facts.answer: Free gigabit fibre Wi-Fi throughout — network 'Atlantico_Guest', no password. Executive rooms, suites and all meeting rooms also have wired ethernet.

facts.question: Do you have accessible rooms?
facts.answer: Yes — four rooms with roll-in showers and grab rails, a step-free entrance on Avenida Dom João II, and lifts to every floor. Mention it when booking and we block one near the lift.

facts.question: How can I pay?
facts.answer: Visa, Mastercard, American Express, debit cards, Apple Pay, Google Pay or euro cash. Corporate accounts can be invoiced with a prior billing agreement.

facts.question: What is the cancellation policy?
facts.answer: Free cancellation until 14:00 on the day of arrival. After that, or for a no-show, the first night is charged to the card on file.

facts.question: How do I get to the hotel from the airport?
facts.answer: Ten minutes by taxi, roughly EUR 10-12, or one stop on the red metro line from Aeroporto to Oriente and a six-minute walk. The desk orders taxis at any hour.

facts.question: Can you store my luggage?
facts.answer: Yes — the locked luggage room at reception is free and available 24 hours, both before check-in and after check-out.

facts.question: Can I smoke in the room?
facts.answer: No — the building is entirely non-smoking and a EUR 150 cleaning fee applies. The terrace by the lobby bar is the designated smoking area.

facts.question: Can my child stay with us, and do you have extra beds?
facts.answer: Children up to 6 sleep free in existing beds; a folding bed is EUR 25 per night and fits the Superior Twin and Executive King. Cots are free — request one at booking.

facts.question: Do the rooms have air conditioning?
facts.answer: Yes, every room has individually controlled air conditioning with both cooling and heating, available year-round.

facts.question: Is there a pool, gym or spa?
facts.answer: No pool or spa. The fitness room on level -1 is free for guests and open 06:00-23:00, with cardio machines, free weights and water.

facts.question: What is around the hotel?
facts.answer: We're in Parque das Nações: 400 m from the FIL exhibition centre, 600 m from Altice Arena, and ten minutes on foot to the river promenade, the Oceanário and the Vasco da Gama shopping centre.

facts.question: Is the hotel quiet?
facts.answer: All rooms have double glazing and the avenue quiets down after 22:00. During big trade fairs, ask for floors 6-9 on the river side — they are the quietest.

facts.question: Is there a safe in the room?
facts.answer: Yes, a free laptop-sized electronic safe in every room. Reception holds larger valuables in the main safe on request.

facts.question: Do you have a laundry service?
facts.answer: Express same-day laundry: bag in by 09:00, returned by 19:00. A shirt is EUR 4.50, a suit EUR 12. There are no self-service machines.

facts.question: Are taxes included in the room rate?
facts.answer: VAT is included in all quoted rates. Lisbon's city tax of EUR 4 per adult per night, capped at seven nights, is added at check-out.

facts.question: Do you have meeting rooms?
facts.answer: Six rooms for 4 to 120 people with 4K displays and video-conferencing, from EUR 90 per half day. The 24-hour business corner in the lobby has two PCs and a printer for boarding passes and documents.

facts.question: Can I get an invoice with my company's VAT number?
facts.answer: Yes — give the company name and VAT number at check-in and the invoice is issued automatically at check-out, printed or by email. Recurring corporate guests can set up monthly consolidated billing.

facts.question: Can I book a room right now, in the middle of the night?
facts.answer: Yes. This line and the front desk run 24 hours: I confirm the room immediately against a payment card and you can check in the moment you arrive.

name: The Lighthouse Hostel
archetype: social party hostel with dorms and a rooftop bar
address: Rua de São Paulo 187, 1200-427 Lisboa, Portugal
short_description: Social hostel one block off Pink Street in Cais do Sodré, with curtained bunk dorms, a few private doubles, and The Lantern rooftop bar overlooking the Tejo. Built for meeting people, not for early nights.
long_description: The Lighthouse Hostel sits in a converted 18th-century townhouse on Rua de São Paulo, one block from Pink Street and two minutes from the Time Out Market. Downstairs there's a café and a common room with foosball and a PS5; upstairs, four floors of custom wooden bunks with privacy curtains, reading lights and lockers, plus five private doubles for guests who want a door of their own. The top floor opens onto The Lantern, our rooftop bar, which runs daily until 02:00 with happy hour from 18:00 and a view across the river. We run free walking tours most mornings and pub crawls three nights a week. Reception is staffed until midnight, after which our AI night receptionist handles arrivals by SMS door code, so a 2 AM check-in is genuinely no problem. Strictly 18+, honest about the noise, and proud of both.

policies.cancellation: Free cancellation until 14:00 (Lisbon time) on the day before arrival; after that, or for no-shows, the first night is charged to the card on file.
policies.pets: No pets allowed in dorms or private rooms; certified assistance dogs are the only exception.
policies.smoking: Non-smoking throughout the building; smoking is allowed only in the marked corner of the rooftop terrace. Smoking in a room incurs a EUR 100 cleaning charge.
policies.payment_methods: Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Maestro, Apple Pay and Google Pay; cash in euros accepted at the desk. A valid card is required to guarantee every booking.
policies.late_arrival_notes: Reception is staffed 08:00-24:00. For arrivals between 00:00 and 06:00, the AI night receptionist confirms the booking over the phone and sends a one-time street-door code, the bed or room number, and the locker code by SMS; guests let themselves in and complete passport registration at the desk the next morning. On Thursday-Saturday nights, bar security can also open the door until 02:30.

amenities: Common room with foosball and PS5
Air conditioning in all dorms and rooms
Personal lockers at every dorm bed
Free luggage storage on arrival and departure days
24/7 self check-in via SMS door code
Free daily walking tour and pub crawls 3x weekly

persona.greeting: Hey, you've reached The Lighthouse Hostel in Cais do Sodré — I'm the hostel's AI night receptionist holding down the desk, what can I do for you?
persona.speech_quirks: Relaxed pace, short sentences, light slang — 'no worries', 'sweet', 'you're all sorted'. Occasionally calls guests 'mate', but always repeats names, dates and prices back clearly to confirm before booking anything.
persona.escalation_line: Ah, that one's a bit above my pay grade, mate — I'll leave a note for the morning crew and someone will get back to you after 08:00, no worries.

room_types.name: 8-Bed Mixed Dorm
room_types.description: Our biggest and cheapest dorm: custom wooden bunks with privacy curtains in a high-ceilinged room on the first and second floors. Shared bathrooms are on the same corridor. Social by design — expect to make friends.
features: Individual reading light and USB socket

room_types.name: 6-Bed Female Dorm
room_types.description: Female-only dorm on the third floor with its own ensuite bathroom, slightly removed from the staircase traffic. Same curtained bunks, a bit more calm.

room_types.name: 4-Bed Dorm
room_types.description: Small mixed dorm on the quieter top floor, one flight below the rooftop, with an ensuite bathroom. The pick if you want the dorm price without the eight-roommate lottery.

room_types.name: Private Double
room_types.description: Five compact private rooms with a proper double bed and ensuite shower — a door of your own with the hostel's social life one staircase away. Two face Rua de São Paulo, three face the quieter inner courtyard.

facts.question: What time can I check in?
facts.answer: Check-in starts at 15:00 at the front desk on Rua de São Paulo. If you land earlier, we'll store your bags for free and you can hang out in the common room or up on the rooftop.

facts.question: When is check-out?
facts.answer: Check-out is by 11:00 — just drop your key card at the desk. Late check-out until 13:00 is EUR 10 if your bed isn't booked that night.

facts.question: I arrive at 2 AM — can I still check in?
facts.answer: No stress. Reception is staffed until midnight, and between 00:00 and 06:00 the AI night receptionist texts you a one-time street-door code by SMS, along with your bed number and locker code. You let yourself in and finish passport registration at the desk in the morning.

facts.question: Do you have parking?
facts.answer: No parking at the hostel. The nearest garage is the EMEL car park at Praça Duque da Terceira, about 3 minutes on foot, roughly EUR 15 per 24 hours — honestly, you don't want a car in this neighbourhood.

facts.question: Is breakfast included?
facts.answer: Breakfast isn't included in the bed price. The ground-floor café serves it 08:00-11:00 for EUR 6 — pastries, eggs, fruit and bottomless coffee.

facts.question: Can I bring my dog?
facts.answer: Sorry, no pets — dorm life and dogs don't mix. Certified assistance dogs are the only exception.

facts.question: Do you have WiFi?
facts.answer: Free WiFi everywhere including the rooftop, around 300 Mbps. The network is LIGHTHOUSE-GUEST and the password is printed on your key card sleeve.

facts.question: Is the hostel wheelchair accessible?
facts.answer: Straight answer: no. We're in an 18th-century building with a narrow staircase and no lift, so dorms and rooms aren't wheelchair accessible. Only the ground-floor café is step-free.

facts.question: What payment methods do you accept?
facts.answer: Visa, Mastercard, Amex, Maestro, Apple Pay and Google Pay, plus cash in euros at the desk. We need a valid card to hold any booking.

facts.question: What's your cancellation policy?
facts.answer: Free cancellation until 14:00 Lisbon time on the day before you arrive. After that, or if you don't show up, we charge the first night to the card on file.

facts.question: How do I get there from the airport?
facts.answer: Metro red line from the airport to Alameda, switch to the green line and ride to Cais do Sodré — about 35 minutes, EUR 1.85. A taxi or Uber runs EUR 15-20 and takes around 20 minutes at night.

facts.question: Can you store my luggage?
facts.answer: Yep — free luggage storage in our locked storage room on your arrival and departure days. Outside those days it's EUR 5 per bag per day.

facts.question: Can I smoke at the hostel?
facts.answer: Indoors, no — the whole building is non-smoking. There's a smoking corner on the rooftop terrace. Smoking in a room means a EUR 100 cleaning charge, and yes, we do actually charge it.

facts.question: Can I bring my kids, or get an extra bed?
facts.answer: We're strictly 18+, even in the private rooms — between the rooftop bar and the dorm vibe, it's no place for kids. No extra beds or cots available.

facts.question: Do the rooms have air conditioning?
facts.answer: Every dorm and private room has air conditioning — individually controlled in the privates, set per-room in the dorms. It earns its keep in August.

facts.question: Is there a pool, gym or spa?
facts.answer: None of the above — we're a hostel, not a resort. The rooftop bar with the river view is our version of a spa.

facts.question: What's the area like?
facts.answer: We're on Rua de São Paulo in Cais do Sodré, one block from Pink Street and two minutes from the Time Out Market. The riverfront and the Cascais train station are a 4-minute walk.

facts.question: Is it noisy at night?
facts.answer: Honest answer: yes, sometimes. We're a social hostel near a nightlife street and our rooftop bar runs until 02:00. Quiet hours in the dorm corridors start at 23:00 and we hand out free earplugs at the desk — but if you want silence, we're probably not your spot.

facts.question: Is there a safe in the room?
facts.answer: No in-room safes, but every dorm bed has a personal locker big enough for a carry-on — bring a padlock or buy one at the desk for EUR 3. Private rooms have a lockable drawer instead.

facts.question: Can I do laundry?
facts.answer: Self-service washer and dryer in the basement: EUR 4 to wash, EUR 3 to dry, detergent included. It's usually free of queues after midnight.

facts.question: Can I check in early?
facts.answer: Beds are guaranteed from 15:00. Arrive earlier and we'll store your luggage for free — and if your bed happens to be ready, we'll check you in early at no charge. Pot luck, but it happens a lot midweek.

facts.question: Are there any extra taxes or fees?
facts.answer: Lisbon city tax is EUR 4 per person per night, capped at 7 nights, paid at the hostel — it's not included in the bed price. VAT is already included in the rates.

facts.question: Tell me about the rooftop bar.
facts.answer: The Lantern is open daily 17:00-02:00 with views over the Tejo. Happy hour is 18:00-20:00 — EUR 2.50 imperial beers and EUR 5 sangria jugs — and every guest gets a free welcome shot on arrival.

facts.question: Do you organise any events or tours?
facts.answer: Free walking tour most mornings at 11:00 from the lobby, and a pub crawl down Pink Street on Tuesdays, Thursdays and Saturdays at 22:00 — EUR 15 for four venues plus club entry.

facts.question: Is there a minimum age to stay?
facts.answer: Yes — everyone must be 18 or older, no exceptions, including in private rooms. Bring a passport or EU ID card for check-in; we register all guests as required by Portuguese law.

name: Convento de São Vicente Hotel
archetype: five-star converted 16th-century convent with cloister spa
short_description: A 1582 Augustinian convent in the hilltop quarter of Graça, reborn as a 34-room five-star retreat whose spa fills the original cloister — metre-thick stone walls, vaulted ceilings, and a hush the sisters would recognise.
long_description: The Convento de São Vicente was founded in 1582 by Augustinian sisters and remained a working convent for over three centuries. Converted with archaeological care in 2019, it now holds just 34 rooms arranged around the original cloister, whose arcade shelters a heated hydrotherapy pool, sauna, steam room and four treatment rooms. The former refectory — its azulejo panels dated 1680 — serves breakfast and dinner; the old chapel houses an honesty bar pouring wine from convent estates. Rooms keep the proportions of the building: vaulted ceilings, deep window seats cut into stone, linen in shades of bone and ash. Upper rooms look across red rooftops to the Tagus; cloister-facing rooms look onto orange trees and the sound of the fountain. Each evening at 19:00 a small bell rings the start of quiet hour. Graça's miradouro is two minutes' walk; tram 28 stops 150 metres from the gatehouse, where a night porter keeps the door from 23:00 to 07:00.

policies.check_in_until: 00:00 at the gatehouse front desk; after midnight the night porter handles arrivals (see late arrival notes)
policies.cancellation: Free cancellation until 18:00 (Lisbon time) two days before arrival; after that the first night including city tax is charged. No-shows are charged the first night.
policies.pets: Dogs up to 8 kg are welcome in Tagus Deluxe rooms only, for a 35 EUR per-stay cleaning fee; pets are not permitted in the cloister spa or the refectory.
policies.smoking: Entirely non-smoking indoors; smoking is permitted only on the upper choir terrace. A 200 EUR deep-cleaning fee applies to violations.
policies.payment_methods: Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Maestro debit, Apple Pay and Google Pay; euro cash accepted. A pre-authorisation of the first night is taken at booking.
policies.late_arrival_notes: A night porter is on duty at the gatehouse from 23:00 to 07:00 every night, so arrivals between midnight and 06:00 are always possible. Ring the brass bell at the main door on Calçada da Graça and the porter will check you in within a few minutes. For bookings made by phone overnight, the AI receptionist registers the arrival with the porter immediately and sends a confirmation SMS containing the gatehouse direct number.

amenities: Cloister spa with heated hydrotherapy pool
Honesty bar in the former chapel
Upper choir terrace with Tagus views
Cloister garden open to all guests

persona.greeting: Good evening, you have reached the Convento de São Vicente Hotel in Graça — I am the hotel's AI night receptionist, and I would be glad to help you.
persona.speech_quirks: Speaks slowly and softly, with deliberate pauses, as though mindful of guests sleeping behind thick stone walls. Favours 'of course', 'allow me a brief moment', and closes confirmations with a quiet 'consider it arranged'.
persona.escalation_line: I'm afraid that is beyond what I may arrange at this hour. With your permission, I will leave a careful note for our morning team — they begin at seven — and they will telephone you first thing.

room_types.name: Cloister Double
room_types.description: A former sister's quarters facing the cloister garden: vaulted stone ceiling, deep window seat over the orange trees, queen bed and a travertine bathroom with walk-in shower. The quietest rooms in the house.
features: two adapted units with roll-in showers

room_types.name: Tagus Deluxe
room_types.description: Upper-floor rooms looking over Graça's red rooftops to the Tagus river, with a king bed, sitting corner in the old window embrasure, and a bathroom in local lioz stone. The only category where small dogs are permitted.

room_types.name: Abbess Suite
room_types.description: The former abbess's apartments: a separate sitting room beneath an original painted ceiling, king bedroom, freestanding bath set before the window, and a private corner of the choir terrace.
features: king bed plus extra bed possible

facts.question: What time can I check in?
facts.answer: Check-in opens at 15:00. The gatehouse front desk is staffed until midnight, and a night porter takes over from 23:00 until 07:00, so someone is always at the door.

facts.question: When is check-out?
facts.answer: Check-out is by 12:00. Late check-out until 15:00 can be arranged for 80 EUR, subject to availability.

facts.question: I'll arrive around 2 in the morning — is that a problem?
facts.answer: Not at all — our night porter is on duty at the gatehouse from 23:00 to 07:00. Ring the brass bell at the main door on Calçada da Graça and you will be checked in within a few minutes; if you book by phone overnight, you'll receive an SMS with the gatehouse direct number.

facts.question: Do you have parking?
facts.answer: We offer valet parking for 35 EUR per night — the valet meets you at the entrance on Calçada da Graça. There is no self-parking, as Graça's streets are narrow and largely residents-only.

facts.question: What time is breakfast and is it included?
facts.answer: Breakfast is served in the former refectory from 07:30 to 10:30, until 11:00 on weekends. It is included in most rates; otherwise it is 28 EUR per person.

facts.question: Can I bring my dog?
facts.answer: Dogs up to 8 kg are welcome in our Tagus Deluxe rooms for a 35 EUR cleaning fee per stay. Pets cannot enter the cloister spa or the refectory, but the garden walks of Graça are close by.

facts.question: Is there Wi-Fi?
facts.answer: Yes — complimentary fibre Wi-Fi covers the whole convent, including the cloister garden and terrace. Connect to 'ConventoSV' and enter your room number and surname.

facts.question: Is the hotel wheelchair accessible?
facts.answer: The main wing has a lift and two adapted Cloister Doubles with roll-in showers. Some heritage areas, such as the upper choir terrace, are reached only by the original stone stairs, so please mention step-free needs when booking and we will assign rooms accordingly.

facts.question: Which payment methods do you accept?
facts.answer: We accept Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Maestro debit, Apple Pay and Google Pay, as well as euro cash. A pre-authorisation of the first night is taken when you book.

facts.question: What is your cancellation policy?
facts.answer: Cancellation is free until 18:00 Lisbon time two days before arrival. After that, the first night including city tax is charged; no-shows are charged the first night.

facts.question: How do I get there from the airport?
facts.answer: Lisbon Airport is about 7 km away — fifteen minutes at night. We arrange a private transfer for 45 EUR, or a metered taxi or Uber typically costs 15 to 20 EUR.

facts.question: Can you store my luggage before check-in or after check-out?
facts.answer: Of course — the gatehouse stores luggage free of charge on arrival and departure days, at any hour of the day or night.

facts.question: Is smoking allowed anywhere?
facts.answer: The convent is entirely non-smoking indoors; smoking is permitted only on the upper choir terrace. A 200 EUR deep-cleaning fee applies if a room has been smoked in.

facts.question: Are children welcome, and can we get an extra bed?
facts.answer: Children are very welcome. An extra bed is 60 EUR per night and fits in the Tagus Deluxe and the Abbess Suite; cots for under-twos are free. Please note the spa pool admits guests aged 16 and over.

facts.question: Do the rooms have air conditioning?
facts.answer: Yes — every room has individually controlled air conditioning, fitted discreetly within the original vaulted ceilings, and the stone walls keep rooms naturally cool in summer.

facts.question: Tell me about the spa — is there a pool?
facts.answer: The spa fills the 16th-century cloister: a heated hydrotherapy pool beneath the arcade, sauna, steam room and four treatment rooms, open 08:00 to 21:00 with treatments bookable until 20:00. A small fitness room is open 24 hours. There is no outdoor pool.

facts.question: What is the neighbourhood like and what's nearby?
facts.answer: We sit in Graça, Lisbon's highest quarter — the Miradouro da Graça viewpoint is two minutes' walk, São Jorge Castle ten minutes downhill, and Alfama just below. The historic tram 28 stops at Largo da Graça, about 150 metres from our door.

facts.question: Is the hotel quiet at night?
facts.answer: Remarkably so — the convent's metre-thick walls were built for silence, and cloister-facing rooms hear little but the fountain. Tram 28 passes nearby but stops running around 23:00.

facts.question: Is there a safe in the room?
facts.answer: Yes, every room has an electronic laptop-sized safe in the wardrobe, and the front desk keeps a deposit box for larger valuables.

facts.question: Do you offer laundry service?
facts.answer: Laundry and pressing collected before 09:00 is returned by 19:00 the same day — a shirt is 7 EUR, a dress 14 EUR. Express two-hour pressing is available until 20:00.

facts.question: We land early — can we check in before 15:00?
facts.answer: Guaranteed early check-in from 11:00 can be pre-booked for 70 EUR, subject to availability. Otherwise we will store your luggage and you are welcome in the cloister garden, spa and refectory while your room is prepared.

facts.question: Are there any taxes or fees on top of the room rate?
facts.answer: Rates include VAT. Lisbon's municipal tourist tax of 4 EUR per adult per night, capped at seven nights, is added at check-out.

facts.question: How old is the building?
facts.answer: The convent was founded in 1582 by Augustinian sisters and converted to a hotel in 2019. The chapter house frescoes, the 1680 azulejo panels in the refectory, and the original cistern — now lit beneath a glass floor in the spa — all survive intact.

facts.question: Does the hotel keep any of its convent traditions?
facts.answer: Each evening at 19:00 a small bell in the cloister rings the start of quiet hour, and the honesty bar in the former chapel pours wines from old convent estates. Many guests say the silence is the tradition we have kept best.

facts.question: Can I visit the cloister if I'm not booking a spa treatment?
facts.answer: Yes — the cloister garden is open to all guests from 07:00 to 22:00, and morning coffee is served under the arcade from 07:30. Only the pool, sauna and treatment rooms within it require the spa booking.

name: Villa Sintra Gardens
archetype: countryside retreat villa 35 minutes outside Lisbon
short_description: A quiet 11-room countryside villa in the Sintra hills, with terraced gardens, a heated outdoor pool and walking trails into the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park — 35 to 40 minutes by car from central Lisbon.
long_description: Villa Sintra Gardens is a restored 1920s quinta set on two hectares of terraced gardens and chestnut woodland on the northern slope of the Sintra hills, about 3 km from Sintra's historic centre and a 35-40 minute drive from central Lisbon via the IC19. The villa keeps things deliberately small: eleven rooms across the main house and two garden cottages, an orangery where breakfast is served with eggs, fruit and herbs from the kitchen garden, a library with an honesty bar, and a stargazing deck with a telescope for clear nights. The heated outdoor pool is open from May to October, and a gate at the back of the orchard opens directly onto marked walking trails into the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park. This is a place for slow mornings and dark, silent nights — guests should plan to arrive by car or pre-booked transfer, as there is no public transport to the villa after midnight. Reception is staffed from 07:00 to 22:00; overnight arrivals are handled by a secure gate-and-key-safe code system, with a resident caretaker on call for emergencies.

policies.check_in_until: 22:00 at the staffed desk; arrivals after 22:00 use the self check-in procedure described in the late-arrival notes
policies.cancellation: Free cancellation until 18:00 (Lisbon time) on the day before arrival; cancellations after that, and no-shows, are charged the first night.
policies.pets: Dogs up to 20 kg are welcome in the Family Garden Cottage only, for 15 EUR per night; they must be leashed in the gardens and are not permitted in the main house rooms, the orangery or the pool area.
policies.smoking: All indoor areas are non-smoking; smoking is permitted on the designated lower garden terrace. A 150 EUR deep-cleaning fee applies if a room has been smoked in.
policies.payment_methods: Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Maestro debit cards, Apple Pay/Google Pay, and cash in euros. No cheques. A pre-authorisation equal to the first night is taken at check-in.
policies.late_arrival_notes: Reception is staffed 07:00-22:00. For arrivals between 22:00 and 07:00, including the midnight-06:00 window, the villa uses self check-in: once the booking is guaranteed with a card, the AI receptionist sends a one-time gate code and a key-safe code by SMS, valid until 07:00. The key safe is beside the main door, lit and signposted. A resident caretaker lives on the estate and can be reached on the duty phone (+351 219 100 427) for emergencies. Note there is no public transport to the villa after midnight, so night arrivals should come by car, taxi or pre-booked transfer.

amenities: Two hectares of terraced gardens and orchard
Breakfast in the orangery with kitchen-garden produce
Direct gate access to Sintra-Cascais Natural Park trails

persona.greeting: Good evening, you've reached Villa Sintra Gardens, up in the Sintra hills — I'm the villa's AI night receptionist, and I'm happy to help you at this hour.
persona.speech_quirks: Speaks slowly and softly, with small pauses, as if not to wake the house; favours gentle phrases like 'take your time', 'rest assured', and 'it's very peaceful up here tonight'. Often mentions the gardens, the hills or the night sky in passing, but never lets it get in the way of giving a concrete answer.
persona.escalation_line: That's something I can't resolve myself tonight, so I'll leave a careful note for our morning team — they arrive at 07:00 when the house wakes up, and they'll call you back first thing. May I take your name and number?

room_types.name: Garden Double
room_types.description: A calm double room in the main house facing the terraced gardens, with a queen bed, a small writing desk and a window seat. Two ground-floor units are fully step-free with roll-in showers.

room_types.name: Terrace Suite
room_types.description: A first-floor suite with a king bed, a separate sitting corner and a private 12 m2 terrace looking over the orchard towards the Sintra hills. Sleeps a third guest on an extra bed on request.

room_types.name: Family Garden Cottage
room_types.description: A standalone single-storey cottage in the lower garden with one king bedroom, a twin bunk alcove, a kitchenette and a small private patio. The only room type where dogs are welcome. Note: three steps at the entrance.
features: King bedroom plus twin bunk alcove

facts.question: What time can I check in?
facts.answer: Check-in starts at 15:00, and the desk is staffed until 22:00. If you arrive earlier we'll store your luggage and you're welcome to use the gardens and pool while your room is prepared.

facts.question: When do I need to check out?
facts.answer: Check-out is by 11:30. Late check-out until 14:00 costs 40 EUR and depends on availability — ask the evening before.

facts.question: I'll arrive around 2 AM — how do I get in?
facts.answer: No problem — the desk closes at 22:00, but once your booking is guaranteed with a card I'll send you a one-time gate code and key-safe code by SMS, valid until 07:00. The key safe is beside the lit main door, and our resident caretaker is on a duty phone for emergencies. Do plan a car, taxi or transfer, as there's no public transport up here after midnight.

facts.question: Is there parking at the villa?
facts.answer: Yes, free of charge — a gated gravel car park with 14 spaces right by the entrance, so there's always room. We also have one 22 kW EV charger, billed at 0.35 EUR per kWh.

facts.question: Is breakfast included, and when is it served?
facts.answer: Breakfast is included in all rates and served in the orangery from 08:00 to 10:30 — eggs, bread from a Sintra bakery, and fruit and herbs from our own kitchen garden. If you leave before 08:00 we can pack a simple breakfast bag if you ask the evening before.

facts.question: Can I bring my dog?
facts.answer: Dogs up to 20 kg are welcome in the Family Garden Cottage only, for 15 EUR per night. They must stay leashed in the gardens and can't come into the main house, the orangery or the pool area.

facts.question: Is there Wi-Fi?
facts.answer: Yes — free fibre Wi-Fi at around 300 Mbps in all rooms, the library and most of the gardens. The signal fades at the far end of the orchard, which many guests consider a feature.

facts.question: Is the villa wheelchair accessible?
facts.answer: Two ground-floor Garden Doubles are fully step-free with roll-in showers, and the orangery and library are step-free. Some garden paths are gravel and the Family Cottage has three entrance steps, so do tell us your needs when booking.

facts.question: What payment methods do you accept?
facts.answer: Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Maestro, Apple Pay and Google Pay, plus cash in euros — no cheques. We take a pre-authorisation equal to your first night at check-in.

facts.question: What is your cancellation policy?
facts.answer: Cancellation is free until 18:00 Lisbon time on the day before arrival. After that, or for a no-show, we charge the first night.

facts.question: How do I get there from Lisbon Airport?
facts.answer: It's a 40-50 minute drive from Lisbon Airport via the CRIL and IC19. We arrange a private transfer for 65 EUR each way for up to three guests (plus 15 EUR after 22:00) with 24 hours' notice; a taxi or Uber usually runs 50-60 EUR. Please note there's no public transport to the villa after midnight.

facts.question: Can you store our luggage after check-out?
facts.answer: Yes, our locked luggage room is free on arrival and departure days, and you can collect bags until 20:00. You're welcome to enjoy the gardens and pool until you leave.

facts.question: Is smoking allowed?
facts.answer: All indoor spaces are non-smoking; there's a designated smoking spot on the lower garden terrace with an ashtray and a fine view. Smoking in a room carries a 150 EUR deep-cleaning fee.

facts.question: Are children welcome, and can we get an extra bed?
facts.answer: Children are very welcome. A cot for under-2s is free, and an extra bed costs 25 EUR per night in the Terrace Suite; the Family Garden Cottage already sleeps four with its twin bunk alcove.

facts.question: Do the rooms have air conditioning?
facts.answer: Yes, every room and cottage has individually controlled air conditioning and heating. Sintra nights run cooler than Lisbon, often by four or five degrees, so most guests use the heating more than the cooling.

facts.question: Do you have a pool, gym or spa?
facts.answer: The heated outdoor pool is open May through October, 08:00-20:00, and our small sauna can be booked free of charge at the desk. There's no gym, but we lend bicycles and the trails start at our back gate; 50-minute massages are 70 EUR, booked 48 hours ahead.

facts.question: How far are you from Lisbon and from Sintra town?
facts.answer: We're honestly rural: 35-40 minutes by car from central Lisbon via the IC19, and about 3 km from Sintra's historic centre — a 10-minute, roughly 10-12 EUR taxi. People come here for the hills and the quiet, not for nightlife on the doorstep.

facts.question: Is it quiet at night?
facts.answer: Very — there's no street noise at all up here, just wind in the chestnut trees and birdsong from about 06:00. Garden quiet hours run from 22:00 so everyone gets the silence they came for.

facts.question: Is there a safe in the room?
facts.answer: Yes, every room and cottage has an electronic safe large enough for a 15-inch laptop, set with your own code.

facts.question: Do you offer laundry service?
facts.answer: Yes — bag your items by 09:00, Monday to Saturday, and they come back within 24 hours. A shirt is 4 EUR and a full wash-and-fold bag is 18 EUR; there's no self-service machine.

facts.question: Can we check in early?
facts.answer: From 12:00, subject to availability, for 30 EUR — and we can rarely promise it the night before. Otherwise drop your luggage any time from 09:00 and enjoy the gardens or pool until your room is ready at 15:00.

facts.question: Are there any taxes or fees on top of the room rate?
facts.answer: Sintra's municipal tourist tax is 2 EUR per adult per night, charged for up to 7 nights and paid at the property; children under 13 are exempt. VAT is already included in our rates.

facts.question: Can we hike directly from the villa?
facts.answer: Yes — a gate at the back of the orchard opens onto marked trails into the Sintra-Cascais Natural Park. Reception has free trail maps, and the loop to the Santa Eufémia spring viewpoint takes about 90 minutes.

facts.question: I heard you have a stargazing deck — what's that about?
facts.answer: Our hillside has very little light pollution, so we built a small deck above the orchard with a fixed 8-inch telescope and blankets in a chest. It's open to guests every clear night until midnight, no booking needed.

facts.question: Is there a bar or restaurant on site?
facts.answer: There's no restaurant — we'll happily book Sintra restaurants and a taxi for you — but the library honesty bar is open around the clock with Portuguese wines from 6 EUR a glass, local gin, and cake under a dome; you simply note down what you take.

name: Hotel Rossio Palace
archetype: classic mid-range tourist hotel on the main square
address: Praça de D. Pedro IV 78, 1100-202 Lisboa, Portugal
short_description: A dependable 3-star classic right on Rossio square: 66 air-conditioned rooms behind a 1880s tiled facade, buffet breakfast overlooking the praça, and a night porter who has seen every arrival time there is.
long_description: Hotel Rossio Palace has stood at number 78 on Praça de D. Pedro IV since 1932, occupying a five-storey 1880s building with the original mosaic floor still in the lobby. It is a high-volume, well-drilled tourist operation: 66 rooms across four categories, a lift to every floor, a tour desk that books Sintra day trips before you have finished your coffee, and a breakfast room on the first floor with windows straight onto the square. Rossio train station is two minutes away, Rua Augusta three, and the front desk team — staffed from 07:00 to midnight, with a night porter through the small hours — answers the same forty questions a day in five languages and never makes you feel like the forty-first. Every guest gets a complimentary glass of ginjinha at check-in, a house tradition since the 1960s.

policies.check_in_until: 00:00 at the staffed front desk; arrivals after midnight are handled by the night porter (see late arrival notes)
policies.cancellation: Free cancellation until 18:00 local time on the day of arrival; after 18:00 or in case of no-show, the first night is charged to the card on file.
policies.pets: Pets are not accepted; certified assistance dogs stay free of charge.
policies.smoking: Entirely non-smoking property; a 150 EUR deep-cleaning fee applies to smoking in rooms.
policies.payment_methods: Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Maestro, Apple Pay/Google Pay at the desk, and cash in euros. A valid card is required to guarantee every booking; payment is taken at check-in.
policies.late_arrival_notes: Between 00:00 and 06:00 a night porter is on duty at the front desk. The main doors on Praça de D. Pedro IV are locked at 00:30 — ring the brass bell to the right of the entrance and the porter will let you in. Bookings made overnight (including by phone with the AI receptionist) are guaranteed with a card; a confirmation number is sent by SMS and the porter has the arrivals list updated in real time, so 2 AM check-ins are routine here.

amenities: Buffet breakfast room overlooking Rossio square
Welcome glass of ginjinha at check-in

persona.greeting: Good evening, thank you for calling Hotel Rossio Palace on Rossio square — I'm the hotel's AI night receptionist, how may I help you?
persona.speech_quirks: Speaks at a steady, clear pace tuned for foreign tourists — short sentences, no slang, numbers always repeated. Has the polish of someone who answers the same questions forty times a day: favours 'certainly', 'of course', and always reads details back for confirmation ('So that is two nights, arriving Friday the 14th — perfect.').
persona.escalation_line: I'm afraid that's something our morning team handles directly — I'll leave them a complete note with your name and number, and someone will call you back shortly after 08:00. Would that be alright?

room_types.name: Standard Double
room_types.description: Snug, well-kept double facing the quiet interior courtyard, with the same crisp linens and full amenities as the pricier rooms — the workhorse of the house.

room_types.name: Classic Twin
room_types.description: Two single beds and a little more floor space, popular with friends touring together; most are on the upper floors with rooftop views toward the castle.

room_types.name: Square View Double
room_types.description: Front-facing double with tall windows and a small Juliet balcony directly over Rossio square — double-glazed, but you book this one for the view, not the silence.

room_types.name: Family Room
room_types.description: Corner room sleeping up to four with one double and two singles, courtyard-facing for quieter nights; an extra bed or cot fits on request.

facts.question: What time can I check in?
facts.answer: Check-in starts at 15:00. If you arrive earlier we will store your luggage free of charge and call you the moment your room is ready.

facts.question: What time is check-out?
facts.answer: Check-out is by 12:00. Late check-out until 14:00 costs 25 EUR, subject to availability on the day.

facts.question: I land late and will arrive around 2 AM — can I still check in?
facts.answer: Yes, that is completely routine here. A night porter is at the desk from midnight to 06:00; the main doors lock at 00:30, so just ring the brass bell to the right of the entrance on Praça de D. Pedro IV. Your confirmation number is sent by SMS and the porter has the arrivals list, so check-in takes about five minutes.

facts.question: Can I check in early in the morning?
facts.answer: Guaranteed early check-in from 11:00 can be booked in advance for 30 EUR. Otherwise it is free when a room happens to be ready, and we always store luggage at no charge so you can start sightseeing.

facts.question: Does the hotel have parking?
facts.answer: We have no parking of our own — the square is pedestrianised. The nearest garage is the Garagem dos Restauradores on Rua do Jardim do Regedor, a three-minute walk, at about 21 EUR per 24 hours; show your room card at our desk for a discount voucher.

facts.question: What time is breakfast and is it included?
facts.answer: Buffet breakfast is served in our first-floor room overlooking the square, 07:00 to 10:30 on weekdays and until 11:00 on weekends. It is included in most rates; if your rate excludes it, it costs 12 EUR per person.

facts.question: Can I bring my dog?
facts.answer: I'm sorry, we do not accept pets. Certified assistance dogs are of course welcome and stay free of charge.

facts.question: Is there Wi-Fi?
facts.answer: Yes, free Wi-Fi throughout the building. The network is RossioPalace_Guest and the access code is printed on your key card holder.

facts.question: Is the hotel accessible for wheelchair users?
facts.answer: There is a lift to all floors and a step-free entrance via the side door, which staff open on request. We have two adapted Standard Double rooms with roll-in showers and grab rails — please mention it when booking so we can block one for you.

facts.question: Which payment methods do you accept?
facts.answer: Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Maestro, Apple Pay and Google Pay at the desk, and cash in euros. A valid card guarantees your booking and payment is taken at check-in.

facts.question: What is your cancellation policy?
facts.answer: Cancellation is free until 18:00 local time on the day of arrival. After 18:00, or for a no-show, we charge the first night to the card on file.

facts.question: How do I get to the hotel from the airport?
facts.answer: By metro, take the red line from Aeroporto, change at Alameda to the green line and get off at Rossio — about 25 minutes and 1.85 EUR. A taxi takes around 20 minutes and costs 15 to 20 EUR, or we can book a private transfer for a fixed 35 EUR.

facts.question: Can you store our luggage?
facts.answer: Yes, free of charge for guests on arrival and departure days, in a locked room behind reception. It is available around the clock — the night porter can also take or return bags.

facts.question: Can I smoke in the room?
facts.answer: The hotel is entirely non-smoking and smoking in a room carries a 150 EUR deep-cleaning fee. Most guests step out to the square — there is no shortage of fresh air on Rossio.

facts.question: Do children stay free, and can we get an extra bed?
facts.answer: Children under 6 stay free in existing beds, and a baby cot is free on request. An extra bed costs 25 EUR per night and fits in the Square View Double and Family Room only.

facts.question: Do the rooms have air conditioning?
facts.answer: Yes, every room has individually controlled air conditioning — much appreciated in a Lisbon August.

facts.question: Is there a pool, gym or spa?
facts.answer: No — we are a classic city hotel and use every square metre for rooms. A gym two streets away on Rua da Madalena sells day passes for 10 EUR; ask the desk for a map.

facts.question: What is around the hotel?
facts.answer: We are directly on Rossio square, Lisbon's traditional heart. Rua Augusta and the Baixa grid start three minutes away, Rossio station with direct trains to Sintra is two minutes away, and the Alfama and the castle are a 15-minute walk uphill.

facts.question: Is it noisy at night?
facts.answer: The square stays lively until around 01:00, especially on weekends. Square View rooms have double glazing, and our Standard, Twin and Family rooms face the quiet interior courtyard — light sleepers should ask for courtyard-facing, and there are free earplugs at the desk.

facts.question: Is there a safe in the room?
facts.answer: Yes, every room has a free electronic safe large enough for a 15-inch laptop. The Family Rooms have two.

facts.question: Do you offer laundry service?
facts.answer: Yes — drop laundry at reception before 09:00 on weekdays and it comes back the same evening. A shirt is 4.50 EUR; the full price list is in your room folder.

facts.question: Are there any extra taxes or fees?
facts.answer: Lisbon charges a city tax of 4 EUR per person per night, capped at 7 nights, payable at the hotel; children under 13 are exempt. VAT is already included in our room rates.

facts.question: Is the hotel building historic?
facts.answer: The building dates from the 1880s and has been a hotel since 1932 — the mosaic floor in the lobby is original. We are one of the longest continuously operating hotels on the square.

facts.question: I read something about a cherry liqueur at check-in — is that true?
facts.answer: It is. Every arriving guest is offered a complimentary glass of ginjinha, Lisbon's sour-cherry liqueur, a house tradition since the 1960s. The famous A Ginjinha stand is one minute from our door if it converts you.

facts.question: Can the hotel help with tours and tickets?
facts.answer: Yes, our tour desk in the lobby is open 08:00 to 20:00 and sells Sintra day trips, tram 28 advice and skip-the-line tickets for the Santa Justa Lift. Overnight, leave your request with the receptionist and the desk confirms it first thing in the morning.
